message.content: Adversaries use Virtualization or Sandbox Evasion to detect and avoid virtual machine environments (VMEs) and sandbox analysis, altering their behavior to evade detection. This technique involves checking for artifacts indicative of VMEs, security monitoring tools, or legitimate user activity. Methods include sleep timers, junk code, and runtime checks. Malware such as Agent Tesla, Bazar, and CozyCar employ these tactics to disengage or conceal their core functions if a VME is detected. The impact includes delayed detection and analysis, allowing adversaries to maintain persistence and execute further malicious activities. Mitigation strategies involve using robust endpoint detection and response (EDR) tools, employing behavioral analysis to detect evasion techniques, and maintaining up-to-date threat intelligence to recognize and counteract these evasion methods.
